description: To 'kasira' is to register a choice or stance formally within a decision procedure. The vote belongs to the participant who casts it; the counting method and threshold belong to the procedure. 'kanu' (choose) can remain private, while a vote enters a shared record or tally.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release into central 'a'. Form a narrow groove along the tongue for unvoiced 's' and hold stressed high-front 'i'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue tip before opening into central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: The velar release of 'ka' narrows into the bright hiss of stressed 'si', then final 'ra' turns the tongue and opens broadly. The movement from open to fine and out again can recall one stance passing through a formal point of registration.
usage_notes: A voter precedes the proposal, candidate, option, or stance selected in the procedure. A nominal 'kasira' is a vote. Exact ballot forms, weights, counting rules, and result categories stay with their source records. 'makelu' (abstain) formally registers no selection.

examples.translation: The members vote on this proposal.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Voting is one available coordination tool whose access and counting rules remain choices the community can inspect.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A vote stays separate from personal agreement and consent. People who abstain or lose the count retain their position.

semantic_domains.activity: formally registering a choice or stance in a procedure
semantic_domains.community: a participant's recorded position within collective decision making
